title: Whats that bird we hear at the end of ep 16
display_title: Whats that bird we hear at the end of ep 16
wikitext:
[]: What's that bird we hear at the end of ep 16?             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jun 13, 2011 8:50 pm For a scene I'm writing in a fanfic, it'd be helpful to know what kind of bird that is that we hear in the final scene of ep 16, when Shinji wakes up in bed. Please and thank you!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 14, 2011 1:02 am You thinking of the cicadas? But there is a bird during sunset scenes, yes. I'm curious, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jun 14, 2011 1:18 am I know what cicadas sound like (and I love them -- they are the voice of summer singing) . The bird in question is the very first thing we hear in the scene where Shinji wakes up, before his eyes have even opened. It continues calling repeatedly throughout the scene. Great mood-setter. If there's a cicada out there that can sound like that I'd be very surprised.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 14, 2011 10:37 am Just listened to the scene. The Eva2 game has that bird sound during the evenings (around 5:00 to 6:30 PM, as the sun is setting) when somebody goes to Misato's apartment. Very pretty call it makes, so I'm curious myself.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Tue Jun 14, 2011 10:56 am Those are Higurashi cicadas. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XEsM-tjtQWw I tried to find a close up video of one singing, but no luck. Here's a pic anyway. You also hear the "min min" cicada in Eva a lot. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k_OemTaXaps Sound familiar?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 14, 2011 10:59 am It IS a cicada?! Whoa... Thanks Zap~!

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jun 14, 2011 11:17 am And I was thinking of what bird you guys were talking about... So cicada sound differently in America? Because that sound in Eva was so familiar to me as I always heard them during the hottest time in Japan (e.g. August).

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Tue Jun 14, 2011 11:27 am There are a few thousand different species all over the world. They all sound different. For instance, the ones we get in Texas (and throughout the hot, humid south) sound like this http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9kmYJHzYEIw http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4yi2-8Mhq3Y (these sound more like the Japanese "min min") http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_VrgQONOKZ0 (this one is for you, Reichu)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 14, 2011 11:30 am symbv wrote: as I always heard them during the hottest time in Japan (e.g. August).

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jun 14, 2011 11:36 am ZapX wrote: T For instance, the ones we get in Texas (and throughout the hot, humid south) sound like this http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9kmYJHzYEIw http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4yi2-8Mhq3Y (these sound more like the Japanese "min min") http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_VrgQONOKZ0 (this one is for you, Reichu)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 14, 2011 11:47 am Don't we also hear tsuku-tsuku boushi in Eva, or am I thinking of other anime (and Yotsuba) ? http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xK7tJezMc7w

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jun 14, 2011 11:57 am symbv wrote: Wow.. very different indeed. And I would only recognize the middle one (Tibicen pruinosa) as cicada. The first one sounds like a big swarm of bees or something similar. On the contrary the third one sounds much quieter...

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Tue Jun 14, 2011 11:57 am The last one is a female; they don't sing. I just threw it in there because Reichu has an ovipositor fetish. I don't remember hearing any tsukutsukuboushi in Eva, but I could be wrong. I haven't watched it in a long time. Those in Thailand sound crazy. Almost like frogs. Check this one out; like a car redlining http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7_XF-26r1Cw The E in EGF stands for entomology.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jun 14, 2011 12:11 pm ZapX wrote: The E in EGF stands for entomology.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jun 14, 2011 7:52 pm ... I'm glad I asked! Funny, I should have known it was a higurashi, I've watched the hell out of higurashi no naku koro ni. As with symbv, out of those videos the Tibicen pruinosa is the only one I identified as a cicada -- those are the kind that are in my area (or sound just like them at least). I've also heard the min min but only in anime. It's a very iconic sound. Thank you again, all.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Wed Jun 15, 2011 8:09 pm I remember when I was in Japan last year and anytime I went into a leafy, green, area the cicadas were singing INCESSANTLY . Damn things nearly drove me insane when I was in Takayama.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Jun 16, 2011 8:16 am This thread creeps me out. Monster giant bugs

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 16, 2011 8:26 am Xard wrote: This thread creeps me out.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Jun 16, 2011 8:30 am Reichu wrote: I was sifting through compost yesterday and I found a larval cicada. It was squirming out of a shed skin. Squirming, squirming, squirming, like a worm, with big black eyes!

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Jun 16, 2011 8:55 am There's nothing scary about cicadas, they mostly just sit there on trees and scream hideously all night. compared to some Japanese bugs they ain't shit
